text.title: Pre-Commit Hooks as the Non-Negotiable Merge Floor Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: Every code repo MUST commit one of: .pre-commit-config.yaml | lefthook.yml | .husky/ — and a setup script that installs it.
rule.rationale: Without the framework, hooks live in individual machines and drift.

rule.statement: Hook set MUST cover at minimum: format, lint, secret-scan, type-check (where applicable). Skipping any reopens the bug class.
rule.rationale: Four are the documented merge floor for AI-written code.

rule.statement: Hooks run BEFORE the commit is created; failures block the commit. Server-side / pre-push fallback is NOT a substitute.
rule.rationale: After-push hooks ship bad commits to history; pre-commit gates them.

rule.statement: CI MUST run the same checks as a safety net (developers can `--no-verify`).
rule.rationale: Local hooks can be bypassed; CI is the merge gate.

rule.statement: Repo MUST provide a single `make hooks` / `./scripts/setup.sh` that installs the framework + hooks.
rule.rationale: Friction kills adoption; one command means everyone has them.

rule.statement: If repo is pure prose / docs-only OR team explicitly rejects local hooks (rare, push back), STOP.
rule.rationale: Skip applies only to truly unsuitable repos.
